title: Zod .optional() vs .nullable() vs .nullish() have different behaviors
body:
These three modifiers produce different TypeScript types and accept different values. Mixing them up causes subtle validation bugs.

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

const schema = z.object({
  a: z.string().optional(),   // string | undefined
  b: z.string().nullable(),   // string | null
  c: z.string().nullish(),    // string | null | undefined
});

schema.parse({ a: undefined, b: null, c: null });       // OK
schema.parse({ a: undefined, b: undefined, c: undefined }); // FAILS on 'b'!
// Error: Expected string | null, received undefined

schema.parse({ b: null }); // OK — 'a' and 'c' are missing (undefined)
```

**Key difference:**
- `.optional()` — field can be omitted or `undefined`, but NOT `null`
- `.nullable()` — field must be present, can be `null`, but NOT `undefined`
- `.nullish()` — field can be omitted, `undefined`, OR `null`

For API input validation, `.nullish()` is usually what you want because JSON APIs may send either `null` (explicit) or omit the field entirely (implicit `undefined`). For database fields, match the column's nullability constraint.

title: Zod .default() only applies when the value is undefined, not null
Zod .transform() runs AFTER validation — errors in transforms are runtime exceptions
Zod .refine() runs even when previous fields fail validation
Zod .strip() vs .passthrough() vs .strict() control unknown keys differently
Zod .coerce converts any value, including invalid ones, into the target type
Zod discriminated unions require a literal discriminator field
Zod .parse() throws on failure — use .safeParse() in production code
Zod schema composition with .extend() creates a new schema — doesn't mutate
Zod recursive schemas require z.lazy() or the schema is infinite
Zod .email() validation is permissive — it allows technically valid but unusual emails
Zod .brand() creates opaque types but doesn't add runtime validation
Zod error messages are generic by default — customize them for UX
Zod .transform() breaks .extend() and .merge() on object schemas
z.infer includes transforms in the output type — use z.input for form types
Zod v4 breaking changes — new import paths and API differences
Zod .preprocess() is deprecated in favor of .pipe() for input transformation
Zod schemas in react-hook-form must use zodResolver — not direct validation
Environment variable validation with Zod must happen at build/startup time
Zod .array() validates all items — performance degrades on large arrays
Zod .string().url() accepts URLs without protocols that you might not expect
Zod schemas are not serializable — cannot send them over the wire or store in DB
Zod .catch() swallows validation errors silently — use only for defaults
Validation middleware must parse raw body before Express json() middleware
Zod .passthrough() doesn't type unknown keys — they're typed as never
Form validation should be both client-side (UX) and server-side (security)
Number inputs from HTML forms are always strings — must coerce on the server
Zod .pick() and .omit() lose refinements applied to the original schema
ZodError: Required at path 'body' — middleware not parsing request body
ZodError flatten() vs format() — choosing the right error structure for your use case
TypeError: schema.parse is not a function — importing Zod schema wrong
Zod union error messages show all branches — confusing for users
Zod error: Invalid enum value at path — forgetting z.nativeEnum for TypeScript enums
Zod refinement error: ctx.addIssue is not a function — using .refine() instead of .superRefine()
react-hook-form validation fires on mount with mode 'all' — causing flash of errors
Zod transform returns NEVER when ctx.addIssue is used without returning z.NEVER
Zod date validation fails on ISO string input — use z.coerce.date() or z.string().datetime()
Zod .min() and .max() on strings count characters, not bytes — emoji and unicode issues
Zod error: Cannot access 'schema' before initialization — circular dependency
Zod parse error gives wrong path for nested array items
TypeScript error: Type 'ZodEffects' not assignable to 'ZodObject' — after adding .refine()
Zod .regex() error message doesn't tell the user what format is expected
